steps.title: Clean all surfaces and pipettes with RNase Away
steps.instruction: Clean all surfaces and pipettes with RNase Away

steps.title: Thaw frozen buffers and primers on ice
steps.instruction: Thaw frozen buffers and primers on ice

steps.title: Prepare 80% EtOH (approximately 45 mL for 96 samples)
steps.instruction: Prepare 80% EtOH (approximately 45 mL for 96 samples)

steps.title: Prepare PEG Solution (22%) by adding...
steps.instruction: Prepare PEG Solution (22%) by adding all ingredients to a 50 mL falcon tube %s %s Do not add the total amount of water until after PEG is completely solubilized

steps.title: Incubate at 40 °C and vortex...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 40 °C and vortex regularly until PEG is completely dissolved

steps.title: Resuspend Sera-Mag Speed Beads carefully and...
steps.instruction: Resuspend Sera-Mag Speed Beads carefully and pipette 1000 µL of bead suspension into a 1.5 mL tube

steps.title: Place on magnet stand and remove storage buffer
steps.instruction: Place on magnet stand and remove storage buffer

steps.title: Add 1000 µL of TE Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 1000 µL of TE Buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA) and resuspend beads

steps.title: Place on magnet stand and remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Place on magnet stand and remove supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step one more time
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step one more time

steps.title: Add 900 µL TE Buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8
steps.instruction: Add 900 µL TE Buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA) and resuspend beads

steps.title: Add the washed Sera-Mag Speed Beads...
steps.instruction: Add the washed Sera-Mag Speed Beads to the PEG Solution (22%) and mix well The final Cleanup Beads (22% PEG) can be aliquoted and stored at 4 °C for up to six months

steps.title: prime-seq can be used on lysate or extracted RNA
steps.instruction: prime-seq can be used on lysate or extracted RNA. It is essential, however, that the samples either have the same input or that they are normalized after the RNA is extracted, otherwise sequencing depth per sample will be impacted. Based on your starting material, please follow one of the following cases:

steps.title: Prepare Bead Binding Buffer (2x) %s...
steps.instruction: Prepare Bead Binding Buffer (2x) %s %s The Bead Binding Buffer (2x) can be stored at Room temperature for up to six months.

steps.title: Prepare Lysis Buffer according to the number of samples
steps.instruction: Prepare Lysis Buffer according to the number of samples. %s %s If sample volume exceeds 25 % of total lysate, use 2x TCL buffer (Qiagen, #1070498) + 1 % β -mercaptoethanol

steps.title: Add 100 µL of Lysis Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of Lysis Buffer to each well of a semi-skirted 96-well PCR plate

steps.title: Add cells or tissue to wells Cells Minimum
steps.instruction: Add cells or tissue to wells Cells Minimum: 100 cells, Optimum: 10,000 cells Make sure that the same number of cells are used for each sample. Large differences between cells will impact distribution of sequencing reads and can potentially affect normalization. Tissue If samples are difficult to lyse they should be homogenized using a tissue homogenizer. Tissue should be a relatively small and not exceed more than 1000 ng of RNA. Tissue samples should be normalized by weight and be the same type of tissue. Large differences between tissue samples will impact distribution of sequencing reads and can potentially affect normalization. If you are unsure if the samples will contain the same amount of RNA, it is best to switch to the " Lysate (variable) " case in Step 13.

steps.title: Transfer 50 µL of lysate to...
steps.instruction: Transfer 50 µL of lysate to a new plate, return one plate immediately to -80 C freezer to save as a backup Conversely, one can prepare two plates during sorting with 50 µL of lysis buffer.

steps.title: Add 1 µL Proteinase K (20...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL Proteinase K (20 mg/mL) and 1 µL EDTA (25 mM) to each well

steps.title: Incubate for 00:15:00 at 50 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:15:00 at 50 °C and then heat inactivate the Proteinase K for 00:10:00 at 75 °C

steps.title: Mix each bulk sample (50 µL...
steps.instruction: Mix each bulk sample (50 µL per well) with 100 µL of Cleanup Beads (22% PEG)

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C,...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp) While binding, prepare DNase I Mix (Step 28)

steps.title: Place on magnet stand until clear...
steps.instruction: Place on magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and then discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 100 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 100 µL of 80% EtOH while the plate is on the magnet. Discard the supernatant After adding EtOH, incubate for 30 s so that all beads are bound to magnet.

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:03:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:03:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Add 5 µL H2O and resuspend...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL H2O and resuspend beads by vortexing vigorously If you encounter bead clumping at this step, try to resuspend the beads by vigorously pipetting the samples. We have generated high quality prime-seq libraries despite heavy clumping.

steps.title: Prepare DNase I Mix %s %s
steps.instruction: Prepare DNase I Mix %s %s

steps.title: Add 15 µL of DNase I...
steps.instruction: Add 15 µL of DNase I Mix and mix by pipetting

steps.title: Incubate DNase I Mix and beads...
steps.instruction: Incubate DNase I Mix and beads for 00:10:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)

steps.title: Heat inactivate the DNase I by...
steps.instruction: Heat inactivate the DNase I by adding 1 µL of EDTA (100 mM) and incubating for 00:05:00 at 65 °C

steps.title: Place plate on magnet stand until...
steps.instruction: Place plate on magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Wash with 100 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 100 µL of 80% EtOH while the plate is on the magnet. Discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying. While drying, prepare Reverse Transcription Mix .

steps.title: Prepare Reverse Transcription Mix %s %s
steps.instruction: Prepare Reverse Transcription Mix %s %s

steps.title: Add 4 µL H2O to the...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL H2O to the beads The 4 µL of water can be combined with the Reverse Transcription Mix by increasing the water in Row 6 from 2.35 µL to 6.35 µL. If working with many samples, or if using a stepper pipette or robot, we find that it is better to add some water separately to prevent the beads from drying too much.

steps.title: Add 5 µL Reverse Transcription Mix
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL Reverse Transcription Mix

steps.title: Add 1 µL of Barcoded oligodT...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of Barcoded oligodT (E3V7NEXT) (10 µM) per well

steps.title: Incubate for 01:30:00 at 42 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 01:30:00 at 42 °C

steps.title: Place the plate on a magnet
steps.instruction: Place the plate on a magnet

steps.title: Pool the supernatant of all wells...
steps.instruction: Pool the supernatant of all wells into a 2 mL tube

steps.title: Add 10 µL of Cleanup Beads...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of Cleanup Beads (22% PEG) per sample for a 1:1 ratio (e.g. 240 µL for 24 samples) The EDTA in the Cleanup Beads (22% PEG) will inactivate the RT.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to allow binding of the cDNA onto beads

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 1 mL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 1 mL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet, discard the supernatant Volume of EtOH should be adjusted depending on the number of samples. More samples will require more EtOH to cover the beads completely.

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute the beads in 17 µL of UltraPure Water
steps.instruction: Elute the beads in 17 µL of UltraPure Water

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and transfer to a new PCR tube or plate

steps.title: Add 2 µL of ExoI Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of ExoI Buffer (10x) and 1 µL of Exonuclease I . Incubate as follows: %s %s

steps.title: Mix each sample (20 µL per...
steps.instruction: Mix each sample (20 µL per well) with 16 µL of Cleanup Beads (22% PEG) for a 1:0.8 ratio

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to allow binding of the cDNA onto beads

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 50 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 50 µL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet, discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute the beads in 20 µL of UltraPure Water
steps.instruction: Elute the beads in 20 µL of UltraPure Water

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and transfer to a new PCR tube or plate

steps.title: Prepare Pre Amplification Mix %s %s
steps.instruction: Prepare Pre Amplification Mix %s %s

steps.title: Add 30 µL Pre Amplification Mix to sample
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL Pre Amplification Mix to sample

steps.title: Incubate the Pre Amplification PCR as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate the Pre Amplification PCR as follows: %s %s Adjust the number of cycles based on input (sample number, cell number, or concentration). As a rule of thumb we assume big cells like embryonic stem cells to contain 10 pg of total RNA and small cells like T-cells ~ 1-2 pg As a general guide we recommend: %s %s

steps.title: Mix sample with 40 µL Clean...
steps.instruction: Mix sample with 40 µL Clean Up Beads (22% PEG) for a ratio of 1:0.8

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 100 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 100 µL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet, discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute cDNA in 10 µL UltraPure Water
steps.instruction: Elute cDNA in 10 µL UltraPure Water

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and transfer to a new PCR tube or plate Stopping Point. Samples can be safely stored at -20 °C and protocol can be continued at a later date.

steps.title: Quantify the cDNA using the Quant-iT...
steps.instruction: Quantify the cDNA using the Quant-iT PicoGreen dsDNA assay kit or equivalent Qubit following the manufacturer's protocol. Use 1 μl of clean cDNA for quantification.

steps.title: Quality check the cDNA using the...
steps.instruction: Quality check the cDNA using the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer with High Sensitivity DNA Analysis Kits . Passing the cDNA quality check does not guarantee that the data will be of high quality, however, if the cDNA fails the quality check it will usually not yield good libraries and will therefore generate lower quality data.

steps.title: Before starting, read the library preparation...
steps.instruction: Before starting, read the library preparation section carefully as there are a few steps that are very time sensitive.

steps.title: Prepare Fragmentation Mix %s %s Ensure...
steps.instruction: Prepare Fragmentation Mix %s %s Ensure that the Ultra II FS Reaction Buffer is completely thawed. If a precipitate is seen in the buffer, pipette up and down several times to break it up, and quickly vortex to mix. Place on ice until use. Vortex the Ultra II FS Enzyme Mix for 5-8 seconds prior to use for optimal performance.

steps.title: Vortex the Fragmentation Mix for 00:00:05...
steps.instruction: Vortex the Fragmentation Mix for 00:00:05 and immediately proceed to step 67

steps.title: Incubate the Fragmentation reaction as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate the Fragmentation reaction as follows: %s %s Set heated lid to 75° C. Make sure the lid is at the correct temperature before you start the reaction. Skip the first incubation step once you have added your samples.

steps.title: Prepare Adapter Ligation Mix %s %s
steps.instruction: Prepare Adapter Ligation Mix %s %s

steps.title: Add 6
steps.instruction: Add 6.7 µL Adapter Ligation Mix to each replicate

steps.title: Incubate for 00:15:00 at 20 °C Turn off heated lid
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:15:00 at 20 °C Turn off heated lid

steps.title: Add 37
steps.instruction: Add 37.3 µL Buffer EB to Samples

steps.title: Mix Sample with 26 µL SPRI...
steps.instruction: Mix Sample with 26 µL SPRI select beads We use SPRI Select Beads here instead of our home made 22% Clean Up beads for their guaranteed QCed size selection properties. The volume of SPRI select beads used during library size selection can be adjusted based on desired library size. Optimization for your samples may be required.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)

steps.title: Place the plate on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on the magnet stand until clear and transfer 76 µL supernatant to clean well . Be careful not to discard! This is your sample!

steps.title: Mix supernatant with 10 µL SPRI...
steps.instruction: Mix supernatant with 10 µL SPRI select beads The volume of SPRI select beads used during library size selection can be adjusted based on desired library size. Optimization for your samples may be required.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)

steps.title: Place the plate on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on the magnet stand until clear and discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 150 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 150 µL of 80% EtOH while the plate is on the magnet, discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute samples in 11 µL 0
steps.instruction: Elute samples in 11 µL 0.1X TE (dilute 1X TE Buffer 1:10 in water) for 00:05:00

steps.title: Prepare Lysis Buffer according to the...
steps.instruction: Prepare Lysis Buffer according to the number of samples %s %s

steps.title: Add 300 µL of Lysis Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL of Lysis Buffer to a 2 mL tube This step can be adjusted based on which tissue homogenizer you are using. We have successfully used the Qiagen Tissue Lyzer, which works well with 300 µL of lysate.

steps.title: Weigh the tissue and add to tube
steps.instruction: Weigh the tissue and add to tube

steps.title: Homogenize the tissue, while making sure...
steps.instruction: Homogenize the tissue, while making sure to keep the samples cold We have successfully used the Qiagen Tissue Lyzer, at 50 oscillation for 2 min for tougher tissue and 30 oscillation for 2 min for softer tissue.

steps.title: Dilute the homogenized lysate with additional...
steps.instruction: Dilute the homogenized lysate with additional Lysis Buffer for a total of 100 µL . Ideally, there should be roughly 100-500 ng of RNA in each sample. We have provided a rough guide below for a variety of tissues. However, it is best to optimize this step and perform a qPCR to determine the amount of RNA present in your samples. A B 1 Tissue Weight per 100 µL (mg) 2 Amygdala 3 3 Thalamus 3 4 Midbrain 3 5 Cerebellum 3 6 Pons 3 7 Medulla 3 8 Heart 2 9 Lung 2 10 Spleen 1 11 Small Intestines 1 12 Colon 1 13 Liver 1 14 Kidney 1 15 Bladder 3 16 Testes 2

steps.title: Add 135 µL Isopropanol
steps.instruction: Add 135 µL Isopropanol

steps.title: Shake for 00:05:00 at 20 °C at 1400 RPM
steps.instruction: Shake for 00:05:00 at 20 °C at 1400 RPM

steps.title: Add 70 µL Cleanup Beads (22% PEG)
steps.instruction: Add 70 µL Cleanup Beads (22% PEG)

steps.title: Shake for 00:05:00 at 20 °C at 1400 RPM
steps.instruction: Shake for 00:05:00 at 20 °C at 1400 RPM

steps.title: Place on magnet stand until clear...
steps.instruction: Place on magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and then discard supernatant

steps.title: Add 300 µL Isopropanol
steps.instruction: Add 300 µL Isopropanol

steps.title: Shake for 00:02:00 at 20 °C at 1400 RPM
steps.instruction: Shake for 00:02:00 at 20 °C at 1400 RPM

steps.title: Place on magnet stand until clear...
steps.instruction: Place on magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and then discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 300 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 300 µL of 80% EtOH while the plate is on the magnet. Discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more Drying...
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more Drying can be controlled better if the tubes are briefly spun down before aspirating the supernatant after the second wash.

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:03:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:03:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute Extracted RNA by adding 20...
steps.instruction: Elute Extracted RNA by adding 20 µL H2O and resuspending the beads

steps.title: Shake for 00:05:00 at 20 °C at 1400 RPM
steps.instruction: Shake for 00:05:00 at 20 °C at 1400 RPM

steps.title: Transfer 8 µL of Extracted RNA to 96-well plate
steps.instruction: Transfer 8 µL of Extracted RNA to 96-well plate

steps.title: Prepare DNase I Mix %s %s
steps.instruction: Prepare DNase I Mix %s %s

steps.title: Add 2 µL of DNase I Mix to each well
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of DNase I Mix to each well

steps.title: Incubate DNase I Mix and beads...
steps.instruction: Incubate DNase I Mix and beads for 00:10:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)

steps.title: Heat inactivate the DNase I by...
steps.instruction: Heat inactivate the DNase I by adding 1 µL of EDTA (100 mM) and incubating for 00:05:00 at 65 °C

steps.title: Place plate on magnet stand until...
steps.instruction: Place plate on magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and discard the supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 100 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 100 µL of 80% EtOH while the plate is on the magnet, discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute RNA with 15 µL H2O
steps.instruction: Elute RNA with 15 µL H2O

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and transfer to a new PCR tube or plate Stopping Point : Extracted RNA can be stored at -80 °C until you are ready to proceed to the next steps.

steps.title: Quantify the cDNA using the Quantifluor...
steps.instruction: Quantify the cDNA using the Quantifluor RNA System or equivalent following the manufacturer's protocol. Use 1 µL of RNA for quantification.

steps.title: Normalize the samples so they are all the same concentration
steps.instruction: Normalize the samples so they are all the same concentration. Minimum : 4 ng (1 ng/µL) Maximum : 40 ng (10 ng/µL)

steps.title: Transfer 4 µL of the normalized...
steps.instruction: Transfer 4 µL of the normalized RNA to a new plate. Return the unnormalized RNA to the freezer.

steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of Cleanup Beads (22% PEG) for each sample for a 1:1 ratio (e.g. 240 µL for 24 samples) The EDTA in the Cleanup Beads (22% PEG) will inactivate the RT and make pooling easier due to the color.

steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)

steps.title: Place the plate on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on the magnet stand until clear and discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 150 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 150 µL of 80% EtOH while the plate is on the magnet, discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute samples in 11 µL 0
steps.instruction: Elute samples in 11 µL 0.1X TE (dilute 1X TE Buffer 1:10 in water) for 00:05:00

steps.title: Transfer 10
steps.instruction: Transfer 10.5 µL of samples to clean wells

steps.title: Add 1 µL of Index Primer...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of Index Primer (Nextera i7, 5 uM) to each well This is the unique index that will be used for demultiplexing libraries.

steps.title: Prepare Library PCR Mix by adding 12
steps.instruction: Prepare Library PCR Mix by adding 12.5 µL Although scaled down, there will not be sufficient Q5 Master Mix (M0544L) in the kit. This item will have to be ordered separately.

steps.title: Incuba t e the Library PCR reaction as follows
steps.instruction: Incuba t e the Library PCR reaction as follows: %s %s Adjust the number of cycles based on cDNA input. As a general guide we recommend: %s %s

steps.title: Add 25 µL Buffer EB to Index PCR
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL Buffer EB to Index PCR

steps.title: Mix Index PCR with 26 µL...
steps.instruction: Mix Index PCR with 26 µL SPRI select beads We use SPRI Select Beads here instead of our home made 22% Clean Up beads for their guaranteed QCed size selection properties. The volume of SPRI select beads used during library size selection can be adjusted based on desired library size. Optimization for your samples may be required.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)

steps.title: Place the plate on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on the magnet stand until clear and transfer 76 µL supernatant to clean well . Be careful not to discard! This is your library.

steps.title: Mix supernatant with 10 µL SPRI...
steps.instruction: Mix supernatant with 10 µL SPRI select beads The volume of SPRI select beads used during library size selection can be adjusted based on desired library size. Optimization for your samples may be required.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)

steps.title: Place the plate on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on the magnet stand until clear and discard supernatant.

steps.title: Wash with 150 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 150 µL of 80% EtOH while the plate is on the magnet, discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute in 15 µL UltraPure Water
steps.instruction: Elute in 15 µL UltraPure Water .

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 and then place on magnet until clear
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 and then place on magnet until clear. Transfer eluted library to new well. Stopping point. The libraries can be safely stored at -20 °C until they will be QCed and sequenced.

steps.title: Quantify and quality control the library...
steps.instruction: Quantify and quality control the library using the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer with High Sensitivity DNA Analysis Kits . Bulk libraries often yield high concentrations, which should be diluted to get accurate molarity measurements on the Bioanalyzer. Ideally, do not load more than 2 ng onto the chip. Libraries will typically exceed 1-5 ng/μl concentration

steps.title: Samples should be submitted according to your Sequencing Facility specifications
steps.instruction: Samples should be submitted according to your Sequencing Facility specifications. prime-seq is compatible with Illumina Sequencing. At least 8 cycles are required for the Index Read (i7) and 28 cycles for the Read 1 (BC+UMI). Dual index sequencing can be done when using patterned flowcells. Read 2 (DNA) should be adjusted based on the quality of the genome being mapped to, but for human and mouse 50 cycles is sufficient. Some potential sequencing options: A B C D E F 1 Sequencer Read 1 Read 2 Index Read (i7) Index Read (i5) Kit 2 NovaSeq 28 94 8 8 NovaSeq SP v1.5 100 cycle 3 NextSeq 500/550 28 56 8 0 NextSeq 500/550 HiOut v3 75 cycle 4 NextSeq 1000/2000 28 94 8 8 NextSeq 1000/2000 P2 100 cycle 5 NextSeq 2000 28 52 8 0 NextSeq 2000 P3 50 cycles 6 HiSeq 28 114 8 0 HiSeq 3000/4000 150 cycles

steps.title: Normalize the samples so they are all the same concentration
steps.instruction: Normalize the samples so they are all the same concentration. Minimum : 4 ng (1 ng/µL) Maximum : 40 ng (10 ng/µL)

steps.title: Transfer 4 µL of the normalized...
steps.instruction: Transfer 4 µL of the normalized RNA to a new plate. Return the unnormalized RNA to the freezer.

steps.title: Prepare Reverse Transcription Mix %s %s
steps.instruction: Prepare Reverse Transcription Mix %s %s

steps.title: Add 4 µL H2O The 4...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL H2O The 4 µL of water can be included in the Reverse Transcription Mix by increasing the water in Row 6 from 2.35 µL to 6.35 µL. However, when working with many samples, or using a stepper pipette or robot, we find that it is better to add some water prior to the RT mix to prevent the beads from drying too much.

steps.title: Add 5 µL Reverse Transcription Mix
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL Reverse Transcription Mix

steps.title: Add 1 µL of Barcoded oligodT...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of Barcoded oligodT (E3V7NEXT) (10 µM) per well

steps.title: Incubate for 01:30:00 at 42 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 01:30:00 at 42 °C

steps.title: Pool all wells of one plate...
steps.instruction: Pool all wells of one plate into a 2 mL tube

steps.title: Add 10 µL of Cleanup Beads...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of Cleanup Beads (22% PEG) for each sample for a 1:1 ratio (e.g. 240 µL for 24 samples) The EDTA in the Cleanup Beads (22% PEG) will inactivate the RT and make pooling easier due to the color.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to allow binding of the cDNA onto beads

steps.title: Wash with 1 mL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 1 mL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet. Discard the supernatant Volume of EtOH should be adjusted depending on the number of samples. More samples will require more EtOH to cover the beads completely.

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute the beads in 17 µL of UltraPure Water
steps.instruction: Elute the beads in 17 µL of UltraPure Water

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and transfer to a new PCR tube or plate

steps.title: Add 2 µL of ExoI Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of ExoI Buffer (10x) and 1 µL of Exonuclease I . Incubate as follows: %s %s

steps.title: Mix each sample (20 µL per...
steps.instruction: Mix each sample (20 µL per well) with 16 µL of Cleanup Beads (22% PEG) for a 1:0.8 ratio

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature to allow binding of the cDNA onto beads

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 50 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 50 µL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet, discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute the beads in 20 µL of UltraPure Water
steps.instruction: Elute the beads in 20 µL of UltraPure Water

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and transfer to a new PCR tube or plate

steps.title: Prepare Pre Amplification Mix %s %s
steps.instruction: Prepare Pre Amplification Mix %s %s

steps.title: Add 30 µL Pre Amplification Mix to sample
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL Pre Amplification Mix to sample

steps.title: Incubate the Pre Amplification PCR as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate the Pre Amplification PCR as follows: %s %s Adjust the number of cycles based on input (sample number, cell number, or concentration). As a general guide we recommend: %s %s

steps.title: Mix sample with 40 µL Clean...
steps.instruction: Mix sample with 40 µL Clean Up Beads (22% PEG) for a ratio of 1:0.8

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)

steps.title: Place the tube on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the tube on the magnet stand until clear (~3 min) and discard supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 100 µL of 80%...
steps.instruction: Wash with 100 µL of 80% EtOH while the tube is on the magnet, discard the supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step once more
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step once more

steps.title: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending...
steps.instruction: Air dry beads for 00:05:00 Depending on temperature and humidity, the beads may dry faster. Therefore it is important to regularly check the beads and avoid over-drying.

steps.title: Elute cDNA in 10 µL UltraPure Water
steps.instruction: Elute cDNA in 10 µL UltraPure Water

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at RT and transfer to a new PCR tube or plate Stopping Point. Samples can be safely stored at -20 °C and protocol can be continued at a later date.

steps.title: Quantify the cDNA using the Quant-iT...
steps.instruction: Quantify the cDNA using the Quant-iT PicoGreen dsDNA assay kit or equivalent Qubit following the manufacturer's protocol. Use 1 μl of clean cDNA for quantification.

steps.title: Quality check the cDNA using the...
steps.instruction: Quality check the cDNA using the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer with High Sensitivity DNA Analysis Kits . Passing the cDNA quality check does not guarantee that the data will be of high quality, however, if the cDNA fails the quality check it will usually not yield good libraries and will therefore generate lower quality data.

steps.title: Before starting, read the library preparation...
steps.instruction: Before starting, read the library preparation section carefully as there are a few steps that are very time sensitive.

steps.title: Prepare Fragmentation Mix %s %s Ensure...
steps.instruction: Prepare Fragmentation Mix %s %s Ensure that the Ultra II FS Reaction Buffer is completely thawed. If a precipitate is seen in the buffer, pipette up and down several times to break it up, and quickly vortex to mix. Place on ice until use. Vortex the Ultra II FS Enzyme Mix for 5-8 seconds prior to use for optimal performance.

steps.title: Vortex the Fragmentation Mix for 00:00:05...
steps.instruction: Vortex the Fragmentation Mix for 00:00:05 and immediately proceed to step 70

steps.title: Incubate the Fragmentation reaction as follows
steps.instruction: Incubate the Fragmentation reaction as follows: %s %s Set heated lid to 75° C. Make sure the lid is at the correct temperature before you start the reaction. Skip the first incubation step once you have added your samples.

steps.title: Prepare Adapter Ligation Mix %s %s
steps.instruction: Prepare Adapter Ligation Mix %s %s

steps.title: Add 6
steps.instruction: Add 6.7 µL Adapter Ligation Mix to each replicate

steps.title: Incubate for 00:15:00 at 20 °C Turn off heated lid
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:15:00 at 20 °C Turn off heated lid

steps.title: Add 37
steps.instruction: Add 37.3 µL Buffer EB to Samples

steps.title: Mix Samples with 26 µL SPRI...
steps.instruction: Mix Samples with 26 µL SPRI select beads We use SPRI Select Beads here instead of our home made 22% Clean Up beads for their guaranteed QCed size selection properties. The volume of SPRI select beads used during library size selection can be adjusted based on desired library size. Optimization for your samples may be required.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at 20 °C, (Room Temp)

steps.title: Place the plate on the magnet...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on the magnet stand until clear and transfer 76 µL supernatant to clean well . Be careful not to discard! This is your sample!

steps.title: Mix supernatant with 10 µL SPRI...
steps.instruction: Mix supernatant with 10 µL SPRI select beads The volume of SPRI select beads used during library size selection can be adjusted based on desired library size. Optimization for your samples may be required.

steps.title: Elute samples in 11 µL 0

steps.title: Transfer 10
steps.instruction: Transfer 10.5 µL of sample to clean wells

steps.title: When running the protocol for the...
steps.instruction: When running the protocol for the first time prepare Cleanup Beads (see end of the protocol)!

steps.title: Prepare PEG Solution (22%) by adding...
steps.instruction: Prepare PEG Solution (22%) by adding all ingredients to a 50 mL falcon tube %s %s Do not add the total amount of water until after PEG is completely solubilized

steps.title: Incubate at 40 °C and vortex...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 40 °C and vortex regularly until PEG is completely dissolved

steps.title: Resuspend Sera-Mag Speed Beads carefully and...
steps.instruction: Resuspend Sera-Mag Speed Beads carefully and pipette 1000 µL of bead suspension into a 1.5 mL tube

steps.title: Place on magnet stand and remove storage buffer
steps.instruction: Place on magnet stand and remove storage buffer

steps.title: Add 1000 µL of TE Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 1000 µL of TE Buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA) and resuspend beads

steps.title: Place on magnet stand and remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Place on magnet stand and remove supernatant

steps.title: Repeat wash step one more time
steps.instruction: Repeat wash step one more time

steps.title: Add 900 µL TE Buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8
steps.instruction: Add 900 µL TE Buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA) and resuspend beads

steps.title: Add the washed Sera-Mag Speed Beads...
steps.instruction: Add the washed Sera-Mag Speed Beads to the PEG Solution (22%) and mix well The final Cleanup Beads (22% PEG) can be aliquoted and stored at 4 °C for up to six months

steps.title: Prepare PEG Solution (22%) by adding...
steps.instruction: Prepare PEG Solution (22%) by adding all ingredients to a 50 mL falcon tube %s %s Do not add the total amount of water until after PEG is completely solubilized
